summary: Ukrainian forces used FPV drones to destroy a Russian TOS-1A MLRS after it was initially damaged by a Bulava loitering munition, and also struck two Russian soldiers on motorcycles during an assault. In the Kostiantynivka direction, the 36th Marine Brigade survived an FPV drone attack, with footage showing a drone detector alerting them to the threat. Russian forces reported 234 targets destroyed on April 21, 2026, including Ukrainian personnel, fixed-wing UAVs, and armored vehicles, while Turkish-made Skydagger HUNTER interceptors entered serial production with speeds up to 320 km/h and manual terminal guidance.
